title: Cypher planner's no-pattern branch must independently re-apply ORDER BY / SKIP / LIMIT
description: QueryPlanner::plan_query collects ORDER BY/LIMIT/SKIP into shared locals while walking query.clauses, but those locals are only converted into Sort/Skip/Limit operators by plan_execution_strategy, called only when the query has a MATCH/MERGE pattern. Any no-pattern query shape (CALL proc() YIELD col RETURN col ORDER BY col, standalone CALL ... YIELD ... ORDER BY with no RETURN, bare RETURN/UNWIND without MATCH) falls into a separate no-pattern branch that built Project/Aggregate + Limit but never Sort — ORDER BY was silently dropped. SKIP had no handling anywhere in the planner at all (confirmed as a separate, pre-existing, system-wide gap via a MATCH ... ORDER BY ... SKIP control test), and was deliberately left unfixed for the pattern-based path — only wired into the no-pattern branch to match the narrower task scope.
whenToUse: Audit every terminal branch of plan_query (plan_execution_strategy for pattern-based queries, the patterns.is_empty() && !return_items.is_empty() branch, and the patterns.is_empty() && CallProcedure branch) whenever a new clause-collection local is added to the shared clause-walking loop. A variable being populated does not mean every downstream branch reads it — the compiler will not warn.
whenNotToUse: Do not assume a fix in one no-pattern branch also covers the MATCH/pattern path, or vice versa. Do not silently widen a narrowly-scoped fix (e.g. "procedure YIELD projections") to cover the pattern-based path without a separate, explicitly-scoped task — the blast radius (aggregation slicing, UNION post-processing, WITH pipelines) is much larger.
